title: Tapping for the Specific Loss
subtitle: An EFT sequence for grief around one particular person
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for grief focused on one specific person who has died. The script works through the particular loss rather than grief in the abstract, naming the specific person and what their absence means. Designed to be adapted to whoever the loss is.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s). EFT has been used as a self-regulation tool in grief contexts in a number of practitioner frameworks.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the grief for one specific person is present and you want a practice for that loss in particular, rather than grief in general.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for grief around one specific person. The script uses [their name] as a placeholder. Replace it with the actual name of the person you are grieving before you begin.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I am still grieving [their name] and I don't know when that will ease, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though some days the loss feels as sharp as it did at the beginning, I am open to letting the grief move through me.
Even though I miss [their name] and I don't want to let go of that, I accept where I am today.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: Missing [their name] today.
Side of Eye: The particular space they left behind.
Under Eye: The things I can't tell them anymore.
Under Nose: The ways their absence shows up in ordinary days.
Chin: The grief that comes in waves and without warning.
Collarbone: The world is different without them in it.
Under Arm: Carrying this loss in a world that expects me to be over it.
Top of Head: Loving someone who is gone.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: My love for them is still real. That doesn't go.
Side of Eye: What they gave me is still with me.
Under Eye: I carry them in some way in everything I do.
Under Nose: The grief is the shape of the love. Both are real.
Chin: I can hold the loss and still function. I have been doing it.
Collarbone: [Their name] mattered. That is a good thing to have been true.
Under Arm: I am allowed to still be in this.
Top of Head: I loved them. That remains.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
